query: I just pushed feat(auth): add OAuth2 login with Google. The UserService was updated to support OAuth users alongside existing password users.
Just committed refactor(storage): extract file upload logic from FileService into a dedicated S3FileStorage class.
We just merged a big PR — feat(payments): integrate Stripe for subscription billing. New PaymentService, updated UserService, and a new billing convention.
Triage my latest changes — I pushed a fix to the rate limiter and a new caching layer.
Just pushed fix(api): correct pagination cursor encoding. The cursor was double-encoded in base64.
Why did we move to a service-class pattern for rate limiting?
Capture this decision: we're adopting OpenTelemetry for distributed tracing across all services.
Run a full knowledge audit — the docs might be stale.
I'm designing the retry logic for the payment service. Should I use exponential backoff or fixed intervals?
Help me write a migration to add an index on users.email.
What does the FileService do? I need to understand it before I touch it.
